title: Front-Running Attack: initialize() Can Be Front-Run to Steal All Minted Tokens
reason: For clones, `LamboFactory` performs `clone` and `initialize` in the same transaction flow; an attacker cannot call `initialize` on the just-created clone before factory initialization.

title: Owner Can Drain All vETH From The Protocol Via updateFactory
reason: This is privileged-owner behavior; README explicitly states owner roles are trusted, so this is not a distinct trustless vulnerability.

title: Unlimited Token Minting via createLaunchPad with Zero Validation
reason: Creating new quote tokens per launchpad is intended factory behavior; claim relies on trusted-owner whitelist management rather than an unintended permission bypass.

title: No Slippage Protection Results in Potential Total Loss of Funds
reason: `buyQuote`/`sellQuote` do enforce `minReturn`; users setting `minReturn=0` is caller choice, not a contract-side bypass.

title: Fee Calculation Precision Loss Favors Protocol Over Users
reason: Integer-division rounding dust is expected in Solidity fee math and does not evidence a meaningful systematic overcharge exploit.

title: rebalance ignores `amountOut` and executes OKX swaps with `minReturn = 0`
reason: Although `amountOut` is unused and swap minReturn is zero, `rebalance` reverts unless net WETH balance increases (`profit > 0`), preventing protocol-loss execution.

title: No Slippage Protection in Rebalance Function
reason: Same core issue as above: execution is profit-gated and non-profitable outcomes revert, so reported total-loss claim is unsupported.

title: Missing Access Control on Rebalance Functions
reason: `extractProfit` is `onlyOwner`; open `rebalance` entrypoint does not grant fund withdrawal and is consistent with keeper-style triggering.

title: Approval Reset Allows Potential Race Condition
reason: No concrete exploit path shown; approvals are to fixed integration addresses for WETH/vETH flows, and overwriting allowance here is not a standalone vulnerability.

title: Unverified underlyingToken Can Break VirtualToken
reason: `underlyingToken` is immutable constructor configuration by deployer/trusted owner; this is deployment misconfiguration risk, not an in-protocol exploit vector.

title: Reentrancy Protection on sellQuote Uses msg.sender.call Without Reentrancy Guard
reason: `sellQuote` is protected by `nonReentrant`; callback-based reentry into guarded paths is blocked.

title: Hardcoded external addresses create chain-deployment fund-loss risk
reason: Project context specifies Ethereum-only deployment; hardcoded mainnet integration addresses are intentional chain-locking in this codebase rather than a reportable bug by themselves.
